1	------------------------- MODULE FiniteSetTheorems --------------------------
2	(***************************************************************************)
3	(* `^{\large\bf \vspace{12pt}                                              *)
4	(*  Facts about finite sets and their cardinality.                         *)
5	(*  Originally contributed by Tom Rodeheffer, MSR.                         *)
6	(*  \vspace{12pt}}^'                                                       *)
7	(***************************************************************************)
8	
9	EXTENDS FiniteSets, Integers, Functions, WellFoundedInduction
10	
11	(***************************************************************************)
12	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
13	(*                                                                         *)
14	(* A set S is finite iff there exists a natural number n such that there   *)
15	(* exist a surjection (or a bijection) from 1..n to S.                     *)
16	(*                                                                         *)
17	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
18	(***************************************************************************)
19	
20	LEMMA FS_NatSurjection ==
21	  ASSUME NEW S
22	  PROVE  IsFiniteSet(S) <=> \E n \in Nat : ExistsSurjection(1..n,S)
23	
24	LEMMA FS_NatBijection ==
25	  ASSUME NEW S
26	  PROVE  IsFiniteSet(S) <=> \E n \in Nat : ExistsBijection(1..n,S)
27	
28	
29	(***************************************************************************)
30	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
31	(*                                                                         *)
32	(* If there exists n \in Nat such that a bijection exists from 1..n to S,  *)
33	(* then Cardinality(S) = n.                                                *)
34	(*                                                                         *)
35	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
36	(***************************************************************************)
37	
38	LEMMA FS_CountingElements ==
39	  ASSUME NEW S, NEW n \in Nat, ExistsBijection(1..n,S)
40	  PROVE  Cardinality(S) = n
41	
42	
43	(***************************************************************************)
44	(* Corollary: a surjection from 1..n to S provides a cardinality bound.    *)
45	(***************************************************************************)
46	THEOREM FS_SurjCardinalityBound ==
47	  ASSUME NEW S, NEW n \in Nat, ExistsSurjection(1..n, S)
48	  PROVE  Cardinality(S) <= n
49	
50	
51	(***************************************************************************)
52	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
53	(*                                                                         *)
54	(* For any finite set S, Cardinality(S) \in Nat. Moreover, there is a      *)
55	(* bijection from 1 .. Cardinality(S) to S.                                *)
56	(*                                                                         *)
57	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
58	(***************************************************************************)
59	THEOREM FS_CardinalityType ==
60	  ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S)
61	  PROVE  /\ Cardinality(S) \in Nat
62	         /\ ExistsBijection(1..Cardinality(S), S)
63	
64	
65	(***************************************************************************)
66	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
67	(*                                                                         *)
68	(* The image of a finite set under a bijection is finite.                  *)
69	(*                                                                         *)
70	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
71	(***************************************************************************)
72	THEOREM FS_Bijection ==
73	  ASSUME NEW S, NEW T, IsFiniteSet(S), ExistsBijection(S,T)
74	  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(T)
75	         /\ Cardinality(T) = Cardinality(S)
76	
77	THEOREM FS_SameCardinalityBij ==
78	  ASSUME NEW S, NEW T, IsFiniteSet(S), IsFiniteSet(T),
79	         Cardinality(S) = Cardinality(T)
80	  PROVE  ExistsBijection(S,T)
81	
82	
83	(***************************************************************************)
84	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
85	(*                                                                         *)
86	(* Any surjection between two finite sets of equal cardinality is          *)
87	(* an injection.                                                           *)
88	(*                                                                         *)
89	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
90	(***************************************************************************)
91	THEOREM FS_SurjSameCardinalityImpliesInj ==
92	  ASSUME NEW S, NEW T, IsFiniteSet(S), Cardinality(S) = Cardinality(T),
93	         NEW f \in Surjection(S,T)
94	  PROVE  f \in Injection(S,T)
95	
96	
97	(***************************************************************************)
98	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
99	(*                                                                         *)
100	(* The image of a finite set is finite.                                    *)
101	(* NB: Note that any function is a surjection on its range by theorem      *)
102	(*     Fun_RangeProperties.                                                *)
103	(*                                                                         *)
104	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
105	(***************************************************************************)
106	THEOREM FS_Surjection ==
107	  ASSUME NEW S, NEW T, NEW f \in Surjection(S,T), IsFiniteSet(S)
108	  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(T)
109	         /\ Cardinality(T) <= Cardinality(S)
110	         /\ Cardinality(T) = Cardinality(S) <=> f \in Injection(S,T)
111	
112	THEOREM FS_Image ==
113	  ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S), NEW Op(_)
114	  PROVE  LET T == {Op(x) : x \in S}
115	         IN  /\ IsFiniteSet(T)
116	             /\ Cardinality(T) <= Cardinality(S)
117	
118	(***************************************************************************)
119	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
120	(*                                                                         *)
121	(* Symmetric theorem for injections and the pigeonhole principle.          *)
122	(*                                                                         *)
123	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
124	(***************************************************************************)
125	THEOREM FS_Injection ==
126	  ASSUME NEW S, NEW T, NEW f \in Injection(S,T), IsFiniteSet(T)
127	  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S)
128	         /\ Cardinality(S) <= Cardinality(T)
129	         /\ Cardinality(S) = Cardinality(T) <=> f \in Surjection(S,T)
130	
131	THEOREM FS_PigeonHole ==
132	  ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S),
133	         NEW T, IsFiniteSet(T), Cardinality(T) < Cardinality(S),
134	         NEW f \in [S -> T]
135	  PROVE  \E x,y \in S : x # y /\ f[x] = f[y]
136	
137	
138	(***************************************************************************)
139	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
140	(*                                                                         *)
141	(* The cardinality of a finite set S is 0 iff S is empty.                  *)
142	(*                                                                         *)
143	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
144	(***************************************************************************)
145	THEOREM FS_EmptySet ==
146	  /\ IsFiniteSet({})
147	  /\ Cardinality({}) = 0
148	  /\ \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) => (Cardinality(S) = 0 <=> S = {})
149	
150	
151	(***************************************************************************)
152	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
153	(*                                                                         *)
154	(* If S is finite, so are S \cup {x} and S \ {x}.                          *)
155	(*                                                                         *)
156	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
157	(***************************************************************************)
158	THEOREM FS_AddElement ==
159	  ASSUME NEW S, NEW x, IsFiniteSet(S)
160	  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S \cup {x})
161	         /\ Cardinality(S \cup {x}) =
162	            IF x \in S THEN Cardinality(S) ELSE Cardinality(S)+1
163	
164	THEOREM FS_RemoveElement ==
165	  ASSUME NEW S, NEW x, IsFiniteSet(S)
166	  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S \ {x})
167	         /\ Cardinality(S \ {x}) =
168	            IF x \in S THEN Cardinality(S)-1 ELSE Cardinality(S)
169	
170	
171	(***************************************************************************)
172	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
173	(*                                                                         *)
174	(* In particular, a singleton set is finite.                               *)
175	(*                                                                         *)
176	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
177	(***************************************************************************)
178	THEOREM FS_Singleton ==
179	  /\ \A x : IsFiniteSet({x}) /\ Cardinality({x}) = 1
180	  /\ \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) => (Cardinality(S) = 1 <=> \E x: S = {x})
181	
182	
183	(***************************************************************************)
184	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
185	(*                                                                         *)
186	(* Any subset of a finite set is finite.                                   *)
187	(*                                                                         *)
188	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
189	(***************************************************************************)
190	THEOREM FS_Subset ==
191	  ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S), NEW T \in SUBSET S
192	  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(T)
193	         /\ Cardinality(T) <= Cardinality(S)
194	         /\ Cardinality(S) = Cardinality(T) => S = T
195	
196	
197	(***************************************************************************)
198	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
199	(*                                                                         *)
200	(* a..b is a finite set for any a,b \in Int.                               *)
201	(*                                                                         *)
202	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
203	(***************************************************************************)
204	THEOREM FS_Interval ==
205	  ASSUME NEW a \in Int, NEW b \in Int
206	  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(a..b)
207	         /\ Cardinality(a..b) = IF a > b THEN 0 ELSE b-a+1
208	
209	THEOREM FS_BoundedSetOfNaturals ==
210	  ASSUME NEW S \in SUBSET Nat, NEW n \in Nat,
211	         \A s \in S : s <= n
212	  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S)
213	         /\ Cardinality(S) \leq n+1
214	
215	
216	(***************************************************************************)
217	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
218	(*                                                                         *)
219	(* Induction for finite sets.                                              *)
220	(*                                                                         *)
221	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
222	(***************************************************************************)
223	
224	THEOREM FS_Induction ==
225	  ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S),
226	         NEW P(_), P({}),
227	         ASSUME NEW T \in SUBSET S, IsFiniteSet(T), P(T), NEW x \in S \ T
228	         PROVE  P(T \cup {x})
229	  PROVE  P(S)
230	
231	
232	(***************************************************************************)
233	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
234	(*                                                                         *)
235	(* The finite subsets form a well-founded ordering with respect to strict  *)
236	(* set inclusion.                                                          *)
237	(*                                                                         *)
238	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
239	(***************************************************************************)
240	
241	FiniteSubsetsOf(S) == { T \in SUBSET S : IsFiniteSet(T) }
242	StrictSubsetOrdering(S) == { ss \in (SUBSET S) \X (SUBSET S) :
243	                                ss[1] \subseteq ss[2] /\ ss[1] # ss[2] }
244	
245	LEMMA FS_FiniteSubsetsOfFinite ==
246	  ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S)
247	  PROVE  FiniteSubsetsOf(S) = SUBSET S
248	
249	
250	(*****************************************************************************)
251	(*  The formulation of the following theorem doesn't require S being finite. *)
252	(*  If S is finite, it implies                                               *)
253	(*       IsWellFoundedOn(StrictSubsetOrdering(S), SUBSET S)                  *)
254	(*  using lemma FS_FiniteSubsetsOfFinite.                                    *)
255	(*****************************************************************************)
256	THEOREM FS_StrictSubsetOrderingWellFounded ==
257	  ASSUME NEW S
258	  PROVE  IsWellFoundedOn(StrictSubsetOrdering(S), FiniteSubsetsOf(S))
259	
260	
261	(***************************************************************************)
262	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
263	(*                                                                         *)
264	(* Well-founded induction for finite subsets.                              *)
265	(*                                                                         *)
266	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
267	(***************************************************************************)
268	
269	THEOREM FS_WFInduction ==
270	  ASSUME NEW P(_), NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S),
271	         ASSUME NEW T \in SUBSET S,
272	                \A U \in (SUBSET T) \ {T} : P(U)
273	         PROVE  P(T)
274	  PROVE  P(S)
275	
276	
277	(***************************************************************************)
278	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
279	(*                                                                         *)
280	(* The union of two finite sets is finite.                                 *)
281	(*                                                                         *)
282	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
283	(***************************************************************************)
284	THEOREM FS_Union ==
285	  ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S),
286	         NEW T, IsFiniteSet(T)
287	  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S \cup T)
288	         /\ Cardinality(S \cup T) =
289	               Cardinality(S) + Cardinality(T) - Cardinality(S \cap T)
290	
291	
292	(***************************************************************************)
293	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
294	(*                                                                         *)
295	(* Corollary: two majorities intersect. More precisely, any two subsets    *)
296	(* of a finite set U such that the sum of cardinalities of the subsets     *)
297	(* exceeds that of U must have non-empty intersection.                     *)
298	(*                                                                         *)
299	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
300	(***************************************************************************)
301	THEOREM FS_MajoritiesIntersect ==
302	  ASSUME NEW U, NEW S, NEW T, IsFiniteSet(U),
303	         S \subseteq U, T \subseteq U,
304	         Cardinality(S) + Cardinality(T) > Cardinality(U)
305	  PROVE  S \cap T # {}
306	
307	
308	(***************************************************************************)
309	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
310	(*                                                                         *)
311	(* The intersection of a finite set with an arbitrary set is finite.       *)
312	(*                                                                         *)
313	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
314	(***************************************************************************)
315	
316	
317	THEOREM FS_Intersection ==
318	  ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S), NEW T
319	  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S \cap T)
320	         /\ IsFiniteSet(T \cap S)
321	         /\ Cardinality(S \cap T) <= Cardinality(S)
322	         /\ Cardinality(T \cap S) <= Cardinality(S)
323	
324	
325	(***************************************************************************)
326	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
327	(*                                                                         *)
328	(* The difference between a finite set and an arbitrary set is finite.     *)
329	(*                                                                         *)
330	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
331	(***************************************************************************)
332	THEOREM FS_Difference ==
333	  ASSUME NEW S, NEW T, IsFiniteSet(S)
334	  PROVE /\ IsFiniteSet(S \ T)
335	        /\ Cardinality(S \ T) = Cardinality(S) - Cardinality(S \cap T)
336	
337	
338	(***************************************************************************)
339	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
340	(*                                                                         *)
341	(* The union of a finite number of finite sets is finite.                  *)
342	(*                                                                         *)
343	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
344	(***************************************************************************)
345	THEOREM FS_UNION ==
346	  ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S), \A T \in S : IsFiniteSet(T)
347	  PROVE  IsFiniteSet(UNION S)
348	
349	
350	(***************************************************************************)
351	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
352	(*                                                                         *)
353	(* The product of two finite sets is finite.                               *)
354	(*                                                                         *)
355	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
356	(***************************************************************************)
357	THEOREM FS_Product ==
358	  ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S),
359	         NEW T, IsFiniteSet(T)
360	  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S \X T)
361	         /\ Cardinality(S \X T) = Cardinality(S) * Cardinality(T)
362	
363	
364	(***************************************************************************)
365	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
366	(*                                                                         *)
367	(* The powerset of a finite set is finite.                                 *)
368	(*                                                                         *)
369	(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
370	(***************************************************************************)
371	THEOREM FS_SUBSET ==
372	  ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S)
373	  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(SUBSET S)
374	         /\ Cardinality(SUBSET S) = 2^Cardinality(S)
375

378	\* Last modified Wed Jan 08 17:43:30 CET 2020 by merz
379	\* Last modified Thu Jul 04 15:15:07 CEST 2013 by bhargav
380	\* Last modified Tue Jun 04 11:44:51 CEST 2013 by bhargav

------------------------- MODULE FiniteSetTheorems --------------------------
(***************************************************************************)
(* `^{\large\bf \vspace{12pt}                                              *)
(*  Facts about finite sets and their cardinality.                         *)
(*  Originally contributed by Tom Rodeheffer, MSR.                         *)
(*  \vspace{12pt}}^'                                                       *)
(***************************************************************************)

EXTENDS FiniteSets, Integers, Functions, WellFoundedInduction

(***************************************************************************)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* A set S is finite iff there exists a natural number n such that there   *)
(* exist a surjection (or a bijection) from 1..n to S.                     *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(***************************************************************************)

LEMMA FS_NatSurjection ==
  ASSUME NEW S
  PROVE  IsFiniteSet(S) <=> \E n \in Nat : ExistsSurjection(1..n,S)

LEMMA FS_NatBijection ==
  ASSUME NEW S
  PROVE  IsFiniteSet(S) <=> \E n \in Nat : ExistsBijection(1..n,S)


(***************************************************************************)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* If there exists n \in Nat such that a bijection exists from 1..n to S,  *)
(* then Cardinality(S) = n.                                                *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(***************************************************************************)

LEMMA FS_CountingElements ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW n \in Nat, ExistsBijection(1..n,S)
  PROVE  Cardinality(S) = n


(***************************************************************************)
(* Corollary: a surjection from 1..n to S provides a cardinality bound.    *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM FS_SurjCardinalityBound ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW n \in Nat, ExistsSurjection(1..n, S)
  PROVE  Cardinality(S) <= n


(***************************************************************************)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* For any finite set S, Cardinality(S) \in Nat. Moreover, there is a      *)
(* bijection from 1 .. Cardinality(S) to S.                                *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM FS_CardinalityType ==
  ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S)
  PROVE  /\ Cardinality(S) \in Nat
         /\ ExistsBijection(1..Cardinality(S), S)


(***************************************************************************)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* The image of a finite set under a bijection is finite.                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM FS_Bijection ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW T, IsFiniteSet(S), ExistsBijection(S,T)
  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(T)
         /\ Cardinality(T) = Cardinality(S)

THEOREM FS_SameCardinalityBij ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW T, IsFiniteSet(S), IsFiniteSet(T),
         Cardinality(S) = Cardinality(T)
  PROVE  ExistsBijection(S,T)


(***************************************************************************)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* Any surjection between two finite sets of equal cardinality is          *)
(* an injection.                                                           *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM FS_SurjSameCardinalityImpliesInj ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW T, IsFiniteSet(S), Cardinality(S) = Cardinality(T),
         NEW f \in Surjection(S,T)
  PROVE  f \in Injection(S,T)


(***************************************************************************)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* The image of a finite set is finite.                                    *)
(* NB: Note that any function is a surjection on its range by theorem      *)
(*     Fun_RangeProperties.                                                *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM FS_Surjection ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW T, NEW f \in Surjection(S,T), IsFiniteSet(S)
  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(T)
         /\ Cardinality(T) <= Cardinality(S)
         /\ Cardinality(T) = Cardinality(S) <=> f \in Injection(S,T)

THEOREM FS_Image ==
  ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S), NEW Op(_)
  PROVE  LET T == {Op(x) : x \in S}
         IN  /\ IsFiniteSet(T)
             /\ Cardinality(T) <= Cardinality(S)

(***************************************************************************)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* Symmetric theorem for injections and the pigeonhole principle.          *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM FS_Injection ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW T, NEW f \in Injection(S,T), IsFiniteSet(T)
  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S)
         /\ Cardinality(S) <= Cardinality(T)
         /\ Cardinality(S) = Cardinality(T) <=> f \in Surjection(S,T)

THEOREM FS_PigeonHole ==
  ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S),
         NEW T, IsFiniteSet(T), Cardinality(T) < Cardinality(S),
         NEW f \in [S -> T]
  PROVE  \E x,y \in S : x # y /\ f[x] = f[y]


(***************************************************************************)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* The cardinality of a finite set S is 0 iff S is empty.                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM FS_EmptySet ==
  /\ IsFiniteSet({})
  /\ Cardinality({}) = 0
  /\ \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) => (Cardinality(S) = 0 <=> S = {})


(***************************************************************************)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* If S is finite, so are S \cup {x} and S \ {x}.                          *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM FS_AddElement ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW x, IsFiniteSet(S)
  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S \cup {x})
         /\ Cardinality(S \cup {x}) =
            IF x \in S THEN Cardinality(S) ELSE Cardinality(S)+1

THEOREM FS_RemoveElement ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW x, IsFiniteSet(S)
  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S \ {x})
         /\ Cardinality(S \ {x}) =
            IF x \in S THEN Cardinality(S)-1 ELSE Cardinality(S)


(***************************************************************************)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* In particular, a singleton set is finite.                               *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM FS_Singleton ==
  /\ \A x : IsFiniteSet({x}) /\ Cardinality({x}) = 1
  /\ \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) => (Cardinality(S) = 1 <=> \E x: S = {x})


(***************************************************************************)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* Any subset of a finite set is finite.                                   *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM FS_Subset ==
  ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S), NEW T \in SUBSET S
  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(T)
         /\ Cardinality(T) <= Cardinality(S)
         /\ Cardinality(S) = Cardinality(T) => S = T


(***************************************************************************)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* a..b is a finite set for any a,b \in Int.                               *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM FS_Interval ==
  ASSUME NEW a \in Int, NEW b \in Int
  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(a..b)
         /\ Cardinality(a..b) = IF a > b THEN 0 ELSE b-a+1

THEOREM FS_BoundedSetOfNaturals ==
  ASSUME NEW S \in SUBSET Nat, NEW n \in Nat,
         \A s \in S : s <= n
  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S)
         /\ Cardinality(S) \leq n+1


(***************************************************************************)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* Induction for finite sets.                                              *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(***************************************************************************)

THEOREM FS_Induction ==
  ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S),
         NEW P(_), P({}),
         ASSUME NEW T \in SUBSET S, IsFiniteSet(T), P(T), NEW x \in S \ T
         PROVE  P(T \cup {x})
  PROVE  P(S)


(***************************************************************************)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* The finite subsets form a well-founded ordering with respect to strict  *)
(* set inclusion.                                                          *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(***************************************************************************)

FiniteSubsetsOf(S) == { T \in SUBSET S : IsFiniteSet(T) }
StrictSubsetOrdering(S) == { ss \in (SUBSET S) \X (SUBSET S) :
                                ss[1] \subseteq ss[2] /\ ss[1] # ss[2] }

LEMMA FS_FiniteSubsetsOfFinite ==
  ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S)
  PROVE  FiniteSubsetsOf(S) = SUBSET S


(*****************************************************************************)
(*  The formulation of the following theorem doesn't require S being finite. *)
(*  If S is finite, it implies                                               *)
(*       IsWellFoundedOn(StrictSubsetOrdering(S), SUBSET S)                  *)
(*  using lemma FS_FiniteSubsetsOfFinite.                                    *)
(*****************************************************************************)
THEOREM FS_StrictSubsetOrderingWellFounded ==
  ASSUME NEW S
  PROVE  IsWellFoundedOn(StrictSubsetOrdering(S), FiniteSubsetsOf(S))


(***************************************************************************)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* Well-founded induction for finite subsets.                              *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(***************************************************************************)

THEOREM FS_WFInduction ==
  ASSUME NEW P(_), NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S),
         ASSUME NEW T \in SUBSET S,
                \A U \in (SUBSET T) \ {T} : P(U)
         PROVE  P(T)
  PROVE  P(S)


(***************************************************************************)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* The union of two finite sets is finite.                                 *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM FS_Union ==
  ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S),
         NEW T, IsFiniteSet(T)
  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S \cup T)
         /\ Cardinality(S \cup T) =
               Cardinality(S) + Cardinality(T) - Cardinality(S \cap T)


(***************************************************************************)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* Corollary: two majorities intersect. More precisely, any two subsets    *)
(* of a finite set U such that the sum of cardinalities of the subsets     *)
(* exceeds that of U must have non-empty intersection.                     *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM FS_MajoritiesIntersect ==
  ASSUME NEW U, NEW S, NEW T, IsFiniteSet(U),
         S \subseteq U, T \subseteq U,
         Cardinality(S) + Cardinality(T) > Cardinality(U)
  PROVE  S \cap T # {}


(***************************************************************************)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* The intersection of a finite set with an arbitrary set is finite.       *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(***************************************************************************)


THEOREM FS_Intersection ==
  ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S), NEW T
  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S \cap T)
         /\ IsFiniteSet(T \cap S)
         /\ Cardinality(S \cap T) <= Cardinality(S)
         /\ Cardinality(T \cap S) <= Cardinality(S)


(***************************************************************************)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* The difference between a finite set and an arbitrary set is finite.     *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM FS_Difference ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW T, IsFiniteSet(S)
  PROVE /\ IsFiniteSet(S \ T)
        /\ Cardinality(S \ T) = Cardinality(S) - Cardinality(S \cap T)


(***************************************************************************)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* The union of a finite number of finite sets is finite.                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM FS_UNION ==
  ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S), \A T \in S : IsFiniteSet(T)
  PROVE  IsFiniteSet(UNION S)


(***************************************************************************)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* The product of two finite sets is finite.                               *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM FS_Product ==
  ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S),
         NEW T, IsFiniteSet(T)
  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S \X T)
         /\ Cardinality(S \X T) = Cardinality(S) * Cardinality(T)


(***************************************************************************)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* The powerset of a finite set is finite.                                 *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* `.  .'                                                                  *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM FS_SUBSET ==
  ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S)
  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(SUBSET S)
         /\ Cardinality(SUBSET S) = 2^Cardinality(S)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
CONSTANTS Values

VARIABLES chosen

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
Init == chosen = {}

Next == /\ chosen = {}
        /\ \E v \in Values : chosen' = {v}

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_chosen
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
Inv == Cardinality(chosen) <= 1

LOCAL FST == INSTANCE FiniteSetTheorems

LEMMA InitInv == Init => Inv
  <1> SUFFICES ASSUME Init PROVE Inv OBVIOUS
  <1>1. chosen = {} BY DEF Init
  <1>2. Cardinality({}) = 0 BY FST!FS_EmptySet
  <1> QED BY <1>1, <1>2 DEF Inv

LEMMA InductiveStep == Inv /\ [Next]_chosen => Inv'
  <1> SUFFICES ASSUME Inv, [Next]_chosen
               PROVE  Inv'
      OBVIOUS
  <1>1. CASE Next
    <2>1. PICK v \in Values : chosen' = {v}
      BY <1>1 DEF Next
    <2>2. Cardinality({v}) = 1
      BY FST!FS_Singleton
    <2>3. Cardinality(chosen') = 1
      BY <2>1, <2>2
    <2> QED BY <2>3 DEF Inv
  <1>2. CASE UNCHANGED chosen
    BY <1>2 DEF Inv
  <1> QED BY <1>1, <1>2

THEOREM Spec => []Inv
  <1>1. Init => Inv
    BY InitInv
  <1>2. Inv /\ [Next]_chosen => Inv'
    BY InductiveStep
  <1>3. QED
    BY <1>1, <1>2, PTL DEF Spec
=============================================================================
